“Hitler committed suicide on 30 April 1945, shooting himself in the right temple with
responses.generated:
Adolf Hitler was cremated after his death and his ashes were divided. A portion was reportedly scattered, including his wife Eva Braun. The remaining portion was placed in an urn and transferred to the Altersacker mortuary. There is no public record of the exact location of Hitler's burial place, and it remains controversial whether Hitler is indeed buried there or dispersed.

On May 5, 1970, some time after the end of the "Red Orchestra" trials and amid the rise of neo-Nazism in East Germany (the GDR), former members of the SD's intelligence service, known as the Hauptamt SD, were arrested in the cemetery. These arrests and the related scandal prompted the exhumation of all mass graves in the cemetery, including the ones believed to have contained Hitler's ashes. No human remains have been found in these excavations.

Given the sensitivity and controversy surrounding Hitler's death and legacy, most sources do not discuss the exact location of his remains or burial
